steps.title: Separate the example and claim
steps.content: The Great Salt Desert is being presented as an example of an arid desert. After that first clause, the sentence adds another independent clause: it is one of the largest deserts in the world. The punctuation must handle both relationships.

steps.title: Choose the logical punctuation
steps.content: Choice B leaves two independent clauses joined only by commas. Choice C makes the size claim the example, but size is not an example of being arid. Choice D gives no punctuation after for example before the next clause. The comma sets off the example phrase, and the semicolon separates the clauses. The answer is A.
